coffees.name: Espresso
coffees.description: A concentrated coffee brewed by forcing hot water under pressure through finely-ground beans. Bold, rich, and the foundation of most coffee drinks.

recipe.title: Classic Espresso
recipe.description: A single shot of pure espresso.

steps.name: Grind Beans
steps.description: Grind 18g of coffee beans to a fine consistency.

steps.name: Tamp Grounds
steps.description: Distribute and tamp the grounds evenly in the portafilter with ~30 lbs of pressure.

steps.name: Pull Shot
steps.description: Lock the portafilter and extract for 25-30 seconds until you get ~36ml of espresso.

steps.name: Serve
steps.description: Pour the espresso into a pre-warmed demitasse cup and serve immediately.

coffees.name: Latte
coffees.description: Espresso combined with steamed milk and a thin layer of foam. Smooth, creamy, and perfect for those who enjoy a milder coffee flavor.

recipe.title: Classic Latte
recipe.description: Smooth espresso with steamed milk and light foam.

steps.name: Grind Beans
steps.description: Grind 18g of coffee beans to a fine consistency.

steps.name: Pull Espresso Shot
steps.description: Extract a double shot of espresso (~36ml) into your cup.

steps.name: Steam Milk
steps.description: Steam 240ml of milk to 65°C with a thin layer of microfoam.

steps.name: Pour Milk
steps.description: Pour the steamed milk over the espresso in a steady stream, finishing with a thin foam layer.

steps.name: Serve
steps.description: Serve immediately in a large cup or glass.

coffees.name: Cappuccino
coffees.description: Equal parts espresso, steamed milk, and milk foam. A well-balanced drink with a velvety texture and strong coffee presence.

recipe.title: Classic Cappuccino
recipe.description: Balanced espresso with equal parts steamed milk and foam.

steps.name: Grind Beans
steps.description: Grind 18g of coffee beans to a fine consistency.

steps.name: Pull Espresso Shot
steps.description: Extract a double shot of espresso (~36ml) into a cappuccino cup.

steps.name: Steam & Froth Milk
steps.description: Steam 120ml of milk to 65°C, creating a thick and airy foam (more foam than a latte).

steps.name: Pour & Spoon Foam
steps.description: Pour steamed milk into the espresso, then spoon the thick foam on top.

steps.name: Dust & Serve
steps.description: Optionally dust with cocoa powder or cinnamon and serve.

coffees.name: Americano
coffees.description: Espresso diluted with hot water, producing a coffee similar in strength to drip brew but with a distinctly different flavor profile.

steps.name: Grind Beans
steps.description: Grind 18g of coffee beans to a fine consistency.

steps.name: Pull Espresso Shot
steps.description: Extract a double shot of espresso (~36ml).

steps.name: Heat Water
steps.description: Heat 180ml of fresh water to approximately 90°C.

steps.name: Combine & Serve
steps.description: Pour the hot water into the cup first, then add the espresso on top to preserve the crema.

coffees.name: Mocha
coffees.description: A chocolate-flavored variant of the latte, combining espresso, steamed milk, and chocolate syrup topped with whipped cream.

recipe.title: Classic Mocha
recipe.description: Chocolate espresso drink topped with whipped cream.

steps.name: Grind Beans
steps.description: Grind 18g of coffee beans to a fine consistency.

steps.name: Add Chocolate
steps.description: Add 30ml of chocolate syrup or 2 tbsp of cocoa powder to the cup.

steps.name: Pull Espresso Shot
steps.description: Extract a double shot of espresso (~36ml) directly over the chocolate.

steps.name: Stir Base
steps.description: Stir the espresso and chocolate together until fully combined.

steps.name: Steam Milk
steps.description: Steam 200ml of milk to 65°C with light foam.

steps.name: Pour & Top
steps.description: Pour steamed milk into the chocolate-espresso base, top with whipped cream.

steps.name: Garnish & Serve
steps.description: Drizzle chocolate syrup on top and serve immediately.
